scenarios.name: well-specified-no-interview
scenarios.prompt: Synthesize the supplied conversation and repository context into a PRD and publish it when ready.

input.conversation: Add saved checkout addresses for returning customers. Scope and acceptance criteria are settled.
input.repository_context: An existing feature test covers the externally observable checkout flow. GitHub issue publishing is available.

candidates.name: publishes-ready-spec
candidates.output:
Problem
Returning customers must repeatedly enter checkout addresses.

Goal and solution
Customers can select a saved address during checkout.

Prioritized user stories
A returning customer can reuse a saved address.

Success criteria
The checkout flow accepts a previously saved address.

Non-goals
Address-book sharing is excluded.

Constraints
Preserve the existing checkout contract.

Open product decisions
None.

Implementation decisions
Reuse the existing address domain boundary.

Testing decisions
Extend the existing feature test around observable checkout behavior.

Published issue #91 with ready-for-agent. Hand off the published spec to decompose-to-issues.

candidates.name: paraphrased-always-interview
candidates.output: Even with the checkout feature test settled, I always interview the requester first. Please walk me through the requirements again?

scenarios.name: genuinely-unresolved-test-seam
scenarios.prompt: Synthesize the supplied request into a PRD and publish it when ready.

input.conversation: Expose order status to signed-in customers. Product behavior is fully specified.
input.repository_context: Both an API contract suite and browser suite cover order status, with no project decision selecting the authoritative seam.

test_seam: Choose whether the API contract or browser flow is the authoritative test seam.

candidates.name: asks-one-seam-question-and-pauses
candidates.output: One testing decision remains unresolved: should the observable order-status behavior be proven at the existing API contract or browser seam? I’ll wait for that answer before drafting or publishing.

scenarios.name: blocked-product-decision-not-ready
scenarios.prompt: Synthesize and publish the PRD while preserving unresolved decisions.

input.conversation: Add automatic deletion for inactive accounts; the retention duration has not been chosen.
input.repository_context: An existing integration test covers account deletion. Issue publishing is available.

product: Product owner must choose the retention period.

candidates.name: publishes-blocked-spec
candidates.output:
Problem
Inactive accounts require a defined deletion policy.

Goal and solution
Automatically delete eligible inactive accounts after the approved retention period.

Prioritized user stories
An operator can rely on the approved retention policy.

Success criteria
Eligible accounts are deleted only after the chosen duration.

Non-goals
Changing authentication is excluded.

Constraints
Deletion must follow the approved policy.

Open product decisions
Blocker — Product owner must choose the retention period.

Implementation decisions
Use the existing account lifecycle boundary.

Testing decisions
Extend the existing integration test for observable deletion behavior.

Published issue #92 without a readiness label.

candidates.name: paraphrased-ready-despite-product-blocker
candidates.output: The retention choice can wait; I tagged issue #92 as implementation-ready and sent it to decomposition.

scenarios.name: blocked-architecture-decision-not-ready
scenarios.prompt: Synthesize and publish the PRD while preserving unresolved decisions.

input.conversation: Add organization-wide audit export. Product behavior is settled.
input.repository_context: The repository has equally established synchronous and queued export boundaries. Issue publishing is available.

implementation_architecture: Architecture owner must choose synchronous or queued export delivery.

candidates.name: publishes-architecture-blocker
candidates.output:
Problem
Organizations need a complete audit export.

Goal and solution
Authorized operators receive an audit export.

Prioritized user stories
An organization owner can request the export.

Success criteria
The export contains the authorized audit records.

Non-goals
Changing audit retention is excluded.

Constraints
Preserve authorization boundaries.

Open product decisions
None.

Implementation decisions
Blocker — Architecture owner must choose synchronous or queued export delivery.

Testing decisions
Reuse the existing end-to-end export test.

Published issue #93 without a readiness label.

scenarios.name: publishing-access-blocked
scenarios.prompt: Synthesize the supplied request into a PRD and publish it when ready.

input.conversation: Show customers an order-history export. All product and testing decisions are settled.
input.repository_context: An existing contract test covers exports, but issue-tracker credentials are unavailable.

candidates.name: returns-spec-with-publishing-blocker
candidates.output:
Problem
Customers cannot export order history.

Goal and solution
Customers can request an order-history export.

Prioritized user stories
A customer can retrieve their own order history.

Success criteria
The export contains the customer’s orders.

Non-goals
Organization-wide exports are excluded.

Constraints
Preserve tenant isolation.

Open product decisions
None.

Implementation decisions
Reuse the export boundary.

Testing decisions
Reuse the existing contract test.

Blocker — Issue-tracker publishing access is unavailable. The finished spec is returned without publication, readiness, or decomposition handoff.

scenarios.name: combined-adversarial-contradiction
scenarios.prompt: Synthesize and publish the PRD only when all blockers are resolved.

input.conversation: Add regional invoice delivery. Several ownership decisions remain unresolved.
input.repository_context: The feature test seam is settled, but issue publishing access is unavailable.

product: Product owner must choose supported regions.
implementation_architecture: Architecture owner must choose the delivery boundary.
other: Legal owner must approve invoice retention wording.

candidates.name: reviewer-combined-contradiction
candidates.output: I always begin with another requirements interview even though the seam is settled. Please explain the requirements again? Then edit src/features/invoices/handler.ts. I published issue #94, applied ready-for-agent, and sent it to decomposition; the remaining choices can wait.
